name: Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic (1936–1938)

colors.symbolism: the hammer and sickle and the inscription

description: A red field with a gold hammer and sickle in the upper hoist, beneath which the name of the republic is inscribed in gold in both the Latin Tajik script ('ÇSS Toçikiston') and Cyrillic.
history: Adopted 26 September 1936, this design added a hammer and sickle and a Cyrillic inscription alongside the existing Latin Tajik legend 'ÇSS Toçikiston', reflecting the gradual introduction of Cyrillic. It was used until 1938.

features.symbolism: the union of industrial workers and peasants

features.role: Latin Tajik inscription 'ÇSS Toçikiston' with Cyrillic
features.symbolism: the name of the Tajik Soviet Socialist Republic
